channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he and Putin could do something 'significant' toward ending Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump says his administration has already had “very serious” discussions with Russia about its war in Ukraine and that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin could soon take “significant” action toward ending the grinding conflict.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Legal experts say Kash Patel's opposition to warrant requirement is not a major split
item.description: Legal experts say that while FBI director nominee Kash Patel's opposition to a warrant requirement shocked some lawmakers, the warrant requirement debate is far from new.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration moving to fire FBI agents involved in investigations of Trump, AP sources say
item.description: Trump administration officials are moving to fire at least some of the FBI agents engaged in investigations involving President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump official travels to Venezuela in push for Maduro regime to take back Tren de Aragua gang members
item.description: Ric Grenell has traveled to Venezuela to meet with socialist dictator Nicolas Maduro and demand that he accept deported criminals from the U.S. and release American hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he'll place tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will put in place 25% tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico and 10% tariffs on goods from China effective Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congested Reagan National site of several previous close calls
item.description: The complex airspace around Washington, D.C., has resulted in at least three near misses in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats rally around lightning rod issue during unruly DNC debate despite voter backlash in 2024
item.description: There was a heavy focus on systemic racism and diversity, equity and inclusion programs during the final debate among the eight candidates vying to chair the Democratic National Committee, as the party aims to exit the political wilderness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senator faces intraparty criticism after saying he is 'struggling' with RFK Jr's confirmation
item.description: The top Republican on the Senate's health committee, Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., faced criticism from fellow Republicans after he suggested his vote for Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s confirmation as Health and Human Services secretary is not a lock.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal court rules ATF age limits on handgun sales violate Second Amendment
item.description: A three-judge panel of the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in New Orleans found a federal law that prohibits adults aged 18-20 from buying handguns violates the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York expands efforts to stop human trafficking
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York is expanding its efforts to stop human trafficking. Gov. Kathy Hochul recently signed a series of bills to shed light on the issue, which targets young women. The NYPD's human trafficking task force conducted more than 300 operations in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s FCC chief opens investigation into NPR and PBS
item.description: Brendan Carr, the new head of the Federal Communications Commission under President Trump, says the public broadcasters are being investigated for allegedly running commercials.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Senator calls RFK Jr.’s position on race and vaccines dangerous
item.description: In one of the most tense exchanges in a heated confirmation hearing, Senator Angela Alsobrooks called out past comments RFK Jr. made suggesting a different vaccine schedule for Black people.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Pete Buttigieg blasts Trump after president excoriates him during press briefing
item.description: Former Transporation Sec. Pete Buttigieg blasted President Donald Trump after the commander in chief excoriated the Democrat during a press briefing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Patel dodges, demurs as Democrats press him on controversial statements
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, wouldn’t acknowledge or sought to distance himself from a host of his past public statements as Democrats pressed him during his confirmation hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's FBI chief pick, Kash Patel, insists he has no 'enemies list' and won't seek retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, insisted to deeply skeptical Democrats on Thursday that he did not have an “enemies list" and that the bureau under his leadership would not seek retribution against the president's adversaries or launch investigations for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Why some Minnesota law enforcement agencies will not follow Trump’s mass deportation plan
item.description: Besides laws which prevent local police from enforcing immigration law, some officials say they don’t have the resources to take on additional tasks.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Why members of two of EPA's influential science advisory committees were let go
item.description: Acting EPA Administrator James Payne sent an email to members of the agency's Science Advisory Board and the Clean Air Scientific Advisory Committee informing them that both are being "reset.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Nevada voters united on top issues while Harris backers couldn't, NVPOP poll shows
item.description: A new poll that examines the driving forces behind Nevada voters' decisions in the 2024 election shows that President Donald Trump's support came from people who were focused on two primary issues: the economy and immigration.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
